text: The laparoscopic view shows an inflammatory region surrounding the gastro-jugular anastomosis, with visible redness and swelling in the tissue.
The surgeon evacuates purulent fluid from the abdomen and pelvis as the initial step of the procedure.
The surgeon gently dissects and removes inflammatory adhesions from the liver, revealing the site of perforation in the gastric tissue.
The laparoscopic view shows a perforation less than one centimeter in diameter, surrounded by inflamed tissue.
The surgeon sizes and mobilizes an omental flap without tension, preparing it for placement over the perforation site.
The surgeon places two O-vicryl sutures to secure the omental patch over the perforation site, ensuring stability and coverage of the area.
The surgeon secures the omental patch over the perforation site without closing the hole, avoiding the risk of enlarging the perforation.
The surgeon places a JP drain in the region after inspecting the omental patch, ensuring proper drainage post-repair.
